{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/34315751?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lrandersson/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lrandersson/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lrandersson/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lrandersson/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/lrandersson", "id": 34315751, "login": "lrandersson", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjM0MzE1NzUx", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lrandersson/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lrandersson/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lrandersson/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lrandersson/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lrandersson/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/lrandersson", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-33430", "cvss": {"score": 7.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:R/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 7.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:R/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-732", "name": "Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource"}], "description": "### Impact

If a developer uses Briefcase to produce an Windows MSI installer for a project, and that project is installed for All Users (i.e., per-machine scope), the installation process creates an directory that inherits all the permissions of the parent directory. Depending on the location chosen by the installing user, this may allow a low privilege but authenticated user to replace or modify the binaries installed by the application. If an administrator then runs the altered binary, the binary will run with elevated privileges.

### Patches

The problem is caused by the template used to generate the WXS file for Windows projects. It was fixed with the following PRs:

* beeware/briefcase-windows-app-template#86
* beeware/briefcase-windows-VisualStudio-template#85

These patches have been backported to the templates used in Briefcase 0.3.26, 0.4.0, and 0.4.1. Re-running `briefcase create` on your Briefcase project will result in the updated templates being used.

### Workarounds

The change from beeware/briefcase-windows-app-template#86 can be added to any existing Briefcase .wxs file generated by Briefcase 0.3.24 or later.

### Resources

beeware/briefcase#2759 is a formal bug report of the problem.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00132, "percentile": 0.03082}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-r3r2-35v9-v238", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-03-23T21:40:10Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-r3r2-35v9-v238", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-r3r2-35v9-v238"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-33430"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-03-26T17:16:38Z", "published_at": "2026-03-23T21:40:10Z", "references": ["https://github.com/beeware/briefcase/security/advisories/GHSA-r3r2-35v9-v238", "https://github.com/beeware/briefcase/issues/2759", "https://github.com/beeware/briefcase-windows-VisualStudio-template/pull/85", "https://github.com/beeware/briefcase-windows-app-template/pull/86", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-33430", "https://github.com/pypa/advisory-database/tree/main/vulns/briefcase/PYSEC-2026-27.yaml", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-r3r2-35v9-v238"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/beeware/briefcase/security-advisories/GHSA-r3r2-35v9-v238", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/beeware/briefcase", "summary": "Briefcase: Windows MSI Installer Privilege Escalation via Insecure Directory Permissions ", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-05T14:26:49Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-r3r2-35v9-v238", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "0.3.26", "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "briefcase"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 0.3.0, < 0.3.26"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Briefcase: Windows MSI Installer Privilege Escalation via Insecure Directory Permissions
